title: How to Choose a Commercial Cleaning Company in Newcastle (Without Getting Burned)
excerpt: Most Newcastle businesses get stung by a commercial cleaner at least once — cheap quote, poor service, no accountability. Here's exactly what to check before you sign anything, from insurance to staff turnover to what a proper scope of works looks like.
content:
Getting commercial cleaning wrong costs more than most business owners expect. Not just in the obvious ways — dirty bathrooms, staff complaints — but in the hidden ways. Disputes over what was or wasn't included. Discovering your cleaner isn't insured when something breaks. Starting the whole search process again six months in because the service quietly fell apart.

Here's what to actually look for before you sign anything.

## Don't Start With Price

Every cleaning company will give you a number. The number is not the point. What matters is what's behind it — who's actually doing the cleaning, how they're supervised, and what happens when they don't show up.

Cheap cleaning usually means high staff turnover, minimal training, and an account manager spread across too many sites to care about yours. That's not a bargain. That's a headache waiting six weeks to arrive.

## Check Their Insurance — Properly

Don't accept "yes, we're insured" as an answer. Ask for a certificate of currency and check it yourself. You want to see:

- Public liability — minimum $10 million
- Workers compensation covering their employees
- That the policy is current, not lapsed

If they can't produce this within 24 hours, keep looking. A legitimate operation has this ready.

## Ask About Staff Turnover

This is the question most businesses forget. High turnover means you're constantly breaking in new people who don't know your site, don't know your quirks, and miss things.

Ask directly: "How long have most of your cleaning staff been with you?" A company with stable, long-term staff is worth more than a slightly cheaper one that cycles through casuals every few months. The best commercial cleaners in Newcastle — the ones worth keeping — tend to have the same people on the same sites for years.

## Be Specific About Your Space Before You Get Quotes

A generic quote for a "500m² office" is close to useless. Before you call anyone, document:

- How many bathrooms and toilets
- Kitchen and breakout facilities
- Any specialist areas — server rooms, medical spaces, food prep areas
- Your operating hours and access restrictions
- Floor types (polished concrete, carpet, timber, vinyl)

The more specific you are upfront, the fewer surprises later. And if a company quotes you without asking any of these questions, that tells you something.

## Insist on a Written Scope of Works

Your contract needs to spell out exactly what's included, at what frequency. Not "general cleaning." Specifically:

- Vacuuming — which areas, how often
- Bathroom cleaning and consumable restocking — what's supplied, what you provide
- Kitchen bench and appliance wipe-downs
- Hard floor sweeping and mopping
- Window cleaning — internal vs external, frequency
- What's explicitly excluded (high-pressure washing, external glass, pest control)

No written scope means no accountability. That suits the cleaner. It doesn't suit you.

## Ask How They Handle Problems

Things go wrong in every cleaning contract at some point. What separates a good company from a bad one is how they respond. Find out:

- Who do you call when something's missed — a dedicated contact or a 1300 number?
- What's their turnaround time on complaints?
- Do they have a re-clean process, or do they just apologise and move on?

A company that gives vague answers here probably doesn't have good answers in practice either.

## Call Their References

Ask for two or three references from businesses similar to yours in size and type — not just the testimonials on their website. Then actually call them. The most useful question: "What happens when something goes wrong?"

That one answer tells you more than any sales pitch.

## Know Your Floor Types

If your space has polished concrete, timber, or specialist commercial flooring, make sure your cleaner knows how to care for it. Using the wrong product on the wrong surface causes real, expensive damage. Ask what training their staff have on different floor finishes — particularly relevant if you're in a fitout in Honeysuckle, Hamilton, or one of the newer Broadmeadow commercial precincts where polished concrete is everywhere.

## One More Thing: Local Matters

A Newcastle-based cleaning company with local staff and a local manager you can actually reach is worth more than a national franchise with a call centre. When something goes wrong at your Wickham office at 6am, you want someone who can be there by 7 — not someone logging a ticket.
